payload.query: Recent accounts stress the importance of party leaders in shepherding legislation. Can we infer that party leaders can mete out discipline and deliver votes when needed? Is it possible to envision a unified theory of congressional leadership? Why or why not?

articles.title: Read the Colorado Supreme Court’s Decision Disqualifying Trump From the Ballot
articles.abstract: Colorado’s top court ruled on Tuesday that former President Donald J. Trump is disqualified from holding office again because he engaged in insurrection leading up to the Jan. 6 storming of the Capitol. Here is the full ruling.
articles.snippet: Colorado’s top court ruled on Tuesday that former President Donald J. Trump is disqualified from holding office again because he engaged in insurrection leading up to the Jan. 6 storming of the Capitol. Here is the full ruling.
